objective: Improve product-wide statistics and observability beyond basic service health: add privacy-safe product funnels, business metrics, synthetic checks, SLO alerts, dashboards and reports for VK Bot, VK Mini App, admin/operator APIs, jobs, workers, providers, payments, billing and future website readiness.

default_assumptions_until_user_answers.cloudflare_scope: do not change Cloudflare dashboard/WAF/tunnel state unless explicitly requested in a later task
default_assumptions_until_user_answers.runtime_target: Docker Compose first, Kubernetes-compatible structure where cheap
default_assumptions_until_user_answers.initial_analytics_store: Prometheus/Grafana with low-cardinality counters, gauges and histograms; no separate event warehouse unless explicitly requested
default_assumptions_until_user_answers.privacy_model: collect aggregate and pseudonymous telemetry only when useful; never log or expose raw prompts, full launch params, raw PII, raw provider/payment payloads or private artifact URLs
default_assumptions_until_user_answers.money_actions: do not run live YooKassa money actions without separate explicit confirmation

active_user_definition.answer: A user is active when they created at least one job, including chat text jobs and image/video generation jobs.
active_user_definition.load_notes: Use existing job creation events/counters and aggregate by surface/operation/modality. Do not label metrics by user_id.

conversion_definition.answer: Track all major conversions: launch-to-job, job-to-success, user-to-payment, payment-to-repeat-job and payment-to-ledger.
conversion_definition.load_notes: Counters and histograms with low-cardinality labels are cheap. Avoid per-user/per-job Prometheus labels; compute heavy ratios in Grafana queries or scheduled aggregate jobs.

margin_definition.answer: Agent should design a professional default later: start with revenue/spend separately and add estimated margin only when cost/revenue mapping is reliable.
margin_definition.load_notes: Estimated counters are cheap. Real margin accuracy depends more on accounting model than system load.

segmentation.answer: Agent should choose professional optimized segments: surface, operation, modality, product model alias, provider, result/status, error_class, and paid/free where available.
segmentation.load_notes: Safe if labels stay bounded. Avoid raw model prompts, user ids, job ids, payment ids, URLs, idempotency keys and raw errors.

retention.answer: Implement D1/D7 retention in Prometheus/Grafana only if it stays low load.
retention.load_notes: Exact retention needs user-level aggregation outside Prometheus labels. Preferred MVP: scheduled daily aggregate gauges from DB using indexed job/user timestamps, no per-user metrics.

provider_cost_source.answer: Use real provider billing/cost source when available.
provider_cost_source.load_notes: Poll provider billing APIs on a slow schedule with caching. Never poll per job/request. Keep current estimated spend as fallback.

slo_targets.load_notes: SLOs are Prometheus recording/alert rules over existing counters/histograms. Query complexity must be kept bounded.

report_delivery.load_notes: One scheduled report with aggregate Prometheus queries is cheap. Do not include secrets, raw ids, prompts or payloads.

synthetic_checks.answer: User requested explanation before choosing depth.
synthetic_checks.load_notes: Endpoint health probes are very cheap. Mock E2E creates test jobs on a slow schedule and must be isolated/non-billable to avoid queue noise.

prompt_analytics.answer: Track prompt character counts and similar buckets without storing prompt text.
prompt_analytics.load_notes: Use histograms/buckets for prompt length/token estimate only. Do not label by prompt content or store prompt samples in metrics/logs.

rules: Per-request counters with bounded labels are low load.
Histograms are acceptable when bucket count and label cardinality are bounded.
Scheduled aggregate DB queries are acceptable when indexed and not more frequent than needed.
Never use Prometheus labels for user_id, job_id, payment_id, prompt text, raw URL, idempotency key or raw error string.
Real provider billing API checks must be scheduled/cached, never called per user request or per job.
Synthetic E2E checks must use mock/non-billable paths and low frequency.

rules.text: After each phase, update this JSON plan: phase/task statuses, discovered gaps, load impact notes, skipped checks and next actions.

After each implementation phase, re-check architecture invariants: no provider calls from VK/Mini App, billing remains ledger-based, inbound events remain idempotent, and observability stack remains private.

After each implementation phase, verify no new metric label, log, trace, alert, report or dashboard exposes secrets, prompts, full launch params, raw PII, raw provider/payment payloads, raw URLs or private artifact URLs.
After each metrics phase, review label cardinality and query cost. Reject user_id/job_id/payment_id/idempotency_key/raw_error/raw_url labels.

After each phase, state expected runtime load impact. If impact is moderate or higher, add mitigation before continuing: aggregation, sampling, recording rules, lower frequency or deferred event-store design.
If a non-obvious repeated error is found, append one sanitized machine-readable entry to .agents/logs/errors.jsonl. Do not log routine progress.
Do not run live YooKassa money actions or real provider spend synthetics unless a later user message explicitly confirms that step.
Commit and push only after relevant checks pass or skipped checks are explicitly justified. Keep commits rollback-friendly and scoped.

checks: JSON parse validation for changed .agents, dashboard and report JSON files
privacy/static check that telemetry body does not include prompt text, launch params, user ids or artifact URLs
manual query sanity where feasible without starting public services
docker compose -p vk-ai-aggregator -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.observability.yml config --quiet
verify .env and runtime secret files are not staged

synthetic_checks_explanation.short_definition: Synthetic checks are automated fake-user checks that periodically verify product paths before a real user reports a problem.
examples: Endpoint-only synthetic: call /healthz, /readyz, local Mini App URL or /miniapp/estimate with safe dev auth and verify status/latency.
Mock E2E synthetic: create a test job through the normal API with mock/non-billable provider, wait for success, verify artifact access, then record only aggregate success/failure.
Mock payment synthetic: use mock payment provider/reconciliation path only, never live YooKassa money, to verify billing state machine logic.
synthetic_checks_explanation.recommended_default: Start with endpoint-only checks plus one low-frequency mock job E2E if it can be isolated from real user dashboards or clearly labeled synthetic=true.
risk_controls: Do not use real provider spend.
Do not use live YooKassa money actions.
Use fixed safe test prompt text only if a prompt is required, and never export it to logs/metrics.
Label synthetic traffic with a bounded label such as source=synthetic, not a user id.
Keep frequency low enough to avoid queue noise.

quality_gates.architecture_review: Read-only inventory only. No provider, billing, idempotency or public observability surface changed.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Inventory did not print secrets. Existing telemetry DTO has no prompt/launch-param/user-id fields; existing Grafana/Prometheus config is private/local in repo scope.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Existing metrics mostly use bounded labels. Existing frontend route label is potentially expandable by client input and must be tightened before adding latency telemetry.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: very_low local repository read load only.

skipped_checks.reason: p0 was read-only inventory with no code/config edits yet

quality_gates.architecture_review: Taxonomy is implemented as metrics helpers and bounded labels only. It does not change provider, billing or inbound control flow.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Allowed labels are surface, journey, step, operation, modality, result, route template, status, flow and source. Forbidden raw ids, prompts, launch params, raw URLs and payloads are not accepted as metric labels.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Frontend route labels were tightened to an allowlist/template mapper before adding latency metrics.
checks_run: JSON parse validation for plan after p0
manual privacy/cardinality review of metrics helper API

quality_gates.architecture_review: Metrics are attached to existing orchestrator, worker, delivery, paymentservice, VK handler and Mini App handler paths. VK Bot and Mini App still create Jobs only; provider calls remain worker-owned; billing remains ledger/capture/topup based.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: No new metric labels contain user_id, job_id, payment_id, idempotency_key, raw_error, raw_url, prompt text, launch params or provider/payment payloads.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: ProductEvents labels are bounded product dimensions. Prompt metrics use histograms by surface/operation/modality only. Credit flow labels are source/flow/result only.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: low runtime impact from counters/histograms on existing events; no new DB queries or external network calls.

quality_gates.architecture_review: Frontend telemetry posts only to the existing Mini App BFF telemetry endpoint. It does not call providers, billing mutation paths or privileged admin APIs.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Telemetry body contains only surface, event_type, screen, route template, status, error_class, step, reason and duration_ms. Launch params remain only in auth header and prompts/request bodies are not copied into telemetry.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Route templates are allowlisted server-side; UI/API latency metrics use bounded labels.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: low client/server load from one launch event and one lightweight API latency event per wrapped request.

skipped_checks.reason: web/miniapp package.json exposes build only; no lint/test scripts are configured

quality_gates.architecture_review: Added local endpoint synthetic runner only. It performs HTTP checks and optional estimate check; no provider spend or live YooKassa money action is triggered by default.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Synthetic runner prints only name, status, ok and duration_ms. It does not print response bodies, launch params or secrets.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: No new metric labels are created by the script; Prometheus blackbox labels remain instance/job only.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: very_low default endpoint checks. Optional estimate check is low and requires explicit flag.
checks_run: manual script review for no body/secret output

skipped_checks.reason: Skipped by default to avoid accidental real provider spend when local worker/provider mode is not proven mock-only
Skipped because current local payment provider may be YooKassa; live money/provider actions require separate confirmation
blackbox config was not changed and blackbox_exporter.exe is not available locally

quality_gates.architecture_review: Added Grafana dashboard JSON only; no public observability surface or scrape target changed.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Dashboard queries aggregate safe metric labels only and do not display ids, prompts, launch params, raw URLs or payloads.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Queries group by bounded labels only. Potentially heavier p95 queries use histogram buckets and bounded dimensions.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: low_to_moderate dashboard query load depending on time range; default range is 6h and no high-cardinality labels are used.

skipped_checks.reason: A consolidated Product Statistics dashboard was added first to avoid duplicating panels across VK Bot/Mini App/Providers/Payments/Jobs dashboards in this pass

quality_gates.architecture_review: Added Prometheus recording/alert rules only. No runtime business path changed.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Alert annotations instruct safe sharing and explicitly forbid secrets, ids, prompts, launch params, payloads and private URLs.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Recording and alert rules aggregate by no labels or bounded labels only.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: low_to_moderate Prometheus query load; rules use sum/increase/histogram_quantile over bounded metrics.

quality_gates.architecture_review: Report changes are query spec only. They do not add live delivery actions or public endpoints.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Report queries are aggregate only and do not select unsafe labels or raw payload data.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: Queries group by bounded labels only.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: low scheduled report load if run daily/weekly against aggregate Prometheus ranges.

quality_gates.architecture_review: Final review found no provider calls from VK Bot or Mini App, no frontend billing mutation, billing remains ledger/capture/topup based, inbound/event paths remain idempotent and observability endpoints remain private in repository config.
quality_gates.privacy_security_review: Diff scan found no committed secrets. .env, .runtime and frontend dist remain ignored. New metrics, alerts, dashboards and reports avoid raw ids, prompts, launch params, raw URLs, raw provider/payment payloads and private artifact URLs.
quality_gates.cardinality_review: No forbidden Prometheus labels were added. New labels are bounded product dimensions or allowlisted route templates.
quality_gates.load_impact_review: Overall expected load is low. Dashboard/alert query load is low_to_moderate on bounded labels; exact DAU/D1/D7 retention remains deferred to a future aggregate/event-store collector to avoid primary DB load.
checks_run: Get-Content ConvertFrom-Json validation for plan/report/dashboard JSON
docker compose -p vk-ai-aggregator -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.observability.yml config --quiet

skipped_checks.reason: blackbox config was not changed and blackbox_exporter.exe is not available locally

inventory_findings.finding: Jobs, payments, providers and frontend metrics exist, but there is no single privacy-safe product funnel metric family that connects entry, estimate, job creation, payment, artifact and delivery steps across surfaces.
inventory_findings.planned_fix: Add bounded product event counters with surface, journey, step, operation, modality and result labels only.

inventory_findings.finding: Prompt analytics are absent. Prompt text must not be logged or labeled, but character-count histograms are safe.
inventory_findings.planned_fix: Add prompt length histograms by surface, operation and modality. Never export prompt text.

inventory_findings.finding: Frontend telemetry sanitizes route strings, but arbitrary authenticated client paths could still create many route label values.
inventory_findings.planned_fix: Restrict frontend telemetry routes to a small allowlist/template mapper before adding latency metrics.

inventory_findings.finding: Exact D1/D7 retention at millions-user scale should not be implemented with user_id Prometheus labels or frequent count(distinct user_id) queries on the hot jobs table.
inventory_findings.planned_fix: Keep Prometheus for low-cardinality signals now. Add a future scheduled aggregate/event warehouse collector for exact DAU/retention.

inventory_findings.finding: Daily report query spec covers jobs/providers/payments, but not unified product funnel, prompt length, credit flow or synthetic state.
inventory_findings.planned_fix: Extend report JSON with aggregate product/funnel queries only.

constraints.text: Prometheus labels, logs, traces, reports and alerts must not include secrets, auth headers, full VK launch params, prompts, raw PII, payment/provider payloads, idempotency keys, raw URLs or private artifact URLs.

Metrics labels must stay low-cardinality: use surface, route template, operation, modality, provider, model alias/code, status/result and sanitized error_class; never use user_id, job_id, payment_id or raw error text.
VK Bot and Mini App must not call AI providers directly. Provider calls stay in worker/provider adapters; billing stays ledger-based; external inbound events stay idempotent.
Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, Alertmanager, OTel and /metrics endpoints must remain private/local/VPN/SSH-only.
Synthetic payment checks must use mock/local paths unless the user separately confirms a live YooKassa test.
Keep progress in this JSON plan and reusable sanitized incidents in .agents/logs/errors.jsonl; do not create redundant human-readable plans unless explicitly requested.

open_questions.question: What counts as an active user?
open_questions.answer: User who created at least one job, including chat, image generation or video generation.

open_questions.question: Which conversions should be tracked?
open_questions.answer: Track all major conversions; expected load is low when implemented as bounded aggregate counters/histograms.

open_questions.question: How should margin be handled?
open_questions.answer: Design intelligently later; start with revenue and spend separately, then add estimated margin when mapping is reliable.

open_questions.question: Which segments should be used?
open_questions.answer: Choose professional optimized segments with bounded cardinality.

open_questions.question: Should D1/D7 retention be implemented in Prometheus/Grafana now?
open_questions.answer: Yes, if it does not create high system load; prefer scheduled aggregate gauges.

open_questions.question: Which source should provider cost use?
open_questions.answer: Use real provider billing/cost source when available, with current estimates as fallback.

open_questions.question: Which SLO targets should be used?
open_questions.answer: Choose targets intelligently for an AI aggregator.

open_questions.question: Where should daily/weekly reports be delivered?
Should synthetic checks be endpoint-only or create mock jobs end-to-end?
open_questions.answer: Explain synthetic checks to user before final implementation depth decision.

open_questions.question: Should anonymized prompt statistics be collected?
open_questions.answer: Yes: character counts and similar buckets only, without prompt text, and only if it stays low load.

phases.load_notes: Read-only repository/config audit. No runtime load except local validation commands.

tasks.text: Inventory existing Prometheus metrics in internal/platform/metrics and all call sites; classify by surface, product journey, billing, provider, infra and security.
Inventory Grafana dashboards and panels; identify panels without backing metrics, dead queries, duplicated views and missing product funnel panels.
Inventory alert rules and map each alert to owner surface, dashboard, runbook action and safe chat payload.
Write gap findings back into this plan as machine-readable backlog items, not a separate human-readable plan.

phases.load_notes: Planning/schema work only. Runtime load starts only when metrics are instrumented.

tasks.text: Define privacy-safe product event taxonomy for launch/auth, estimate, job creation, provider lifecycle, moderation, artifact access, delivery, payments and repeat usage.
Define allowed low-cardinality metric labels and forbidden labels for each metric family.
Normalize error_class values across frontend, API, worker, provider, payment and delivery paths.

phases.load_notes: Counters/histograms on existing code paths are cheap if labels stay bounded. Retention/business aggregates must use scheduled indexed queries, not per-request user-level metrics.

tasks.text: Add Mini App funnel counters/histograms for auth, balance, estimate, create job, chat message, artifact access and payment intent calls.
Add VK Bot product counters for inbound event classes, menu actions, text mode starts, job creation, delivery success/failure and referral actions.
Add end-to-end job lifecycle metrics for created-to-result_ready, created-to-delivered/succeeded, failure class and retry/DLQ paths.
Add aggregate payment/credit metrics: intents created, succeeded, canceled, top-up ledger applied, payment-to-ledger latency and credits flow.
Add provider/model/operation estimated spend and output volume metrics without raw prompts or payloads.

phases.load_notes: Client telemetry must be sampled/coalesced where needed. Events should be coarse and bounded; no prompt or launch-param payloads.

tasks.text: Extend safe frontend telemetry for launch/render timing, API latency buckets, JS error class and payment flow step failures.
Add optional safe buckets for VK platform/client type where available without raw user identifiers or full launch params.
Add tests or static checks ensuring frontend telemetry does not send prompt text, launch params, artifact URLs or user ids in JSON body.

phases.load_notes: Endpoint probes are very low load. Mock E2E can add queue noise if too frequent; run low-frequency, clearly labeled synthetic traffic only.

tasks.text: Add local synthetic probes for API health, Mini App dev URL, provider-webhook readyz and observability targets.
Add safe mock end-to-end check that creates a non-billable/mock job and verifies it reaches success/artifact availability without live provider spend.
tasks.skip_reason: Skipped by default in this pass to avoid accidental real provider spend when local worker/provider mode is not proven mock-only. Endpoint and optional estimate synthetics were added instead.

tasks.text: Add mock payment/reconciliation synthetic checks that do not trigger live YooKassa money actions.
tasks.skip_reason: Skipped because the current local payment provider can be YooKassa. Live/money-like payment synthetic actions require separate explicit confirmation.

phases.load_notes: Dashboard load depends on query ranges/cardinality. Prefer recording rules for expensive SLO/funnel queries.

tasks.text: Create or extend Grafana dashboard for product funnel: launch/auth, estimate, job create, success, artifact access, payment and repeat usage.
Create business dashboard for users, jobs, success rate, payments, credits, provider estimated spend and top sanitized error classes.
Update VK Bot, Mini App, Providers, Payments and Jobs dashboards to expose the new product metrics with consistent naming.
tasks.skip_reason: Skipped in favor of a consolidated Product Statistics dashboard to avoid duplicating panels across existing surface dashboards in this pass.

phases.load_notes: Simple alert rules are low load; burn-rate rules can be moderate. Use recording rules and avoid high-cardinality PromQL.

tasks.text: Add recording rules for availability, job success, provider success, payment-to-ledger latency and user-visible failure rate where Prometheus can support them.
Add SLO/burn-rate or multi-window alerts for API availability, job success, payment backlog, provider degradation and frontend launch failures.
Add alerts for provider spend thresholds, payment conversion anomalies, zero successful jobs, no successful payments and abnormal DLQ growth.

phases.load_notes: Scheduled Telegram reports are cheap if queries are aggregate and limited. Avoid per-user report generation.

tasks.text: Extend daily report query pack with users, jobs, success/fail, payments, credits, provider spend, top incidents, security state and queue/DLQ state.
Ensure reports contain aggregate counts/rates only and never secrets, raw identifiers, prompts, raw payment/provider payloads or private URLs.

phases.load_notes: Validation commands use local CPU/Docker only. Do not run live money/provider actions as validation.

tasks.text: Run gofmt, go test ./... and go vet ./... after backend metric changes.
Run Mini App lint/typecheck/build scripts when frontend telemetry changes.
Validate Prometheus config/rules, blackbox config, Grafana dashboard JSON, report JSON and docker compose config.
Run staged diff secret/privacy scan before commit; verify no metric labels or alert templates expose forbidden data.
Commit and push to fastlife_dev only after relevant checks pass or skipped checks are explicitly justified.

definition_of_done.text: This JSON plan is updated with final task statuses and no obsolete active plan file remains in .agents/plans.
Grafana exposes a product funnel from user entry to job success/artifact/payment for VK Bot and Mini App using safe aggregate metrics.
Dashboards/reports expose aggregate users, jobs, success rate, payments, credits and provider estimated spend without unsafe labels.
Safe local/mock synthetic checks cover core product paths without live provider spend or live YooKassa money actions.
New or updated alerts include Russian summary, impact, dashboard, action and safe_logs guidance.
No secrets, prompts, full launch params, raw PII, raw provider/payment payloads or private artifact URLs are logged, labeled, reported or committed.
Relevant Go, frontend, Prometheus, blackbox, Grafana JSON, report JSON, docker compose and secret-scan checks pass or are explicitly justified.
